title: linaria genistifolia
intro: Linaria genistifolia, also known as butter and eggs or toadflax, is a perennial plant species belonging to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Linaria genistifolia include its ability to soothe sore throats, calm digestive issues, and aid in the relief of fever. Linaria genistifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, although the preparation methods and dosage may vary depending on the intended use and individual needs.
